round1: Round one is over and it's clear most of you were predicting with a dartboard. A stunning four participants, Glenda, Jaclyn, Jake, and Jamie, managed to correctly pick zero series, while the entire pool whiffed on the Wild and Flames upsets. Nathan, however, apparently has a working crystal ball, leading with four correct picks.
round2: Well, Round 2 was a masterclass in bracket destruction, as Chicago's sweep of Minnesota sent nearly everyone's hopes up in smoke. While Marc somehow managed to nail two series, the rest of you clearly spent more time picking team colors than actual winners.
round3: Round 3 is in the books, and while Jake, Derrick, and Jamie managed to pick winners and game counts, the rest of you proved that 'fan' doesn't mean 'prophet.' Congrats to Marc and Stephanie for the clean sweep of incorrect winners – truly a masterclass in futility.
round4: Round 4 is done, and it seems most of you are allergic to being right. Seven of you bet big on Tampa Bay, only to watch them lose to Chicago in 6. Jaclyn, congratulations on being the only one who actually watches hockey.
overall: Well, the 2015 playoffs are officially in the books, and much like the Blackhawks' predictable dominance, our family pool had a clear winner and a spectacular failure. Nathan, with his astounding 50 points from 12 correct teams and 6 game counts, didn't just win; he humiliated the rest of you, making it look like he actually watches hockey instead of just picking team colours. Derrick and Marc had a respectable tussle for second, proving that even mediocrity can be entertaining, while Jamie deserves a nod for squeezing 31 points out of a measly 4 teams – efficient, unlike most of you. And then there's Glenda, who, with her paltry 20 points and a spectacular ability to pick losers, clearly spent more time picking out her game-day snacks than actually researching anything, cementing her place firmly at the bottom of the barrel.
